spec_match.rationale: Models exactly the spec change: pre-v134 cap of 20 aggregatable reports per source is lifted when trigger_context_id is non-null. The fetched backend route emits a real Attribution-Reporting-Register-Trigger header so the comparison isn't purely synthetic.

interactive.rationale: Range input for conversion count + checkbox toggling trigger_context_id; both drive a side-by-side bar render and dispatch an async fetch to the server route that returns accepted/dropped counts.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises a probe that builds registrations and compares report eligibility; page delivers that with the pre/post columns and a live counter.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles backend failure with an empty catch + fallback to synthetic counts, but does not surface that fallback to the user (no banner). No demo of trigger_context_id set to an empty string vs null (both should leave the cap in place per spec).

references.rationale: see-also links chromestatus.com/feature/5079048977645568 and the WICG attribution-reporting-api spec.

design_system.rationale: All colours via variables, the .bar.drop uses opacity 0.35 over accent-rose for the dropped slots, button-less interactive surface so the global black/ivory button rule isn't relevant.

openQuestions.title: Surface backend-fallback state visibly
openQuestions.detail: If the fetch to ../trigger-context-id-builder/register-trigger throws (offline, route 500), the catch swallows the error and the page keeps the synthetic pre-computed counts. Add a probe-style banner that flips to 'backend unreachable — synthetic counts' so visitors can tell whether the numbers came from the real route or the JS-side simulation.

openQuestions.title: Demo trigger_context_id = empty string vs null
openQuestions.detail: Per spec, the cap is only lifted when trigger_context_id is non-null AND non-empty. An empty string still hits the cap. Add a third radio state ('set to ""') so the spec subtlety is visible — right now the checkbox conflates null with present.

openQuestions.title: Show the noise-budget consequence
openQuestions.detail: Lifting the per-source report cap shifts the privacy-budget accounting onto contribution noise. The spec link explains; the page should at least surface a 'why no cap is OK for privacy' line so visitors don't think the API just got laxer.

openQuestions.title: Render the header the backend actually emits
openQuestions.detail: The fetch response is parsed for acceptedReports/droppedReports, but the actual Attribution-Reporting-Register-Trigger header echoed by the server is never displayed. Add a code block that shows the response header so the demo proves the server is emitting the right wire format.

summary: Strong baseline concept: probe + range + real backend fetch into a side-by-side comparison. The two gaps are spec subtlety (null vs '' for trigger_context_id) and surfacing the real header the backend emits.
